```{r}
          depth_colors_w["Strat_EPI"],depth_colors_w["Strat_DCL"],depth_colors_w["Strat_TRM"],
          depth_colors_w["Strat_HYP"])

#change dynamically
label_start   <- wx1 + wx2 + 0.01
label_spacing <- 0.03

season_locx <- label_start + c(0, 2)                            * label_spacing  # 2 labels
lake_locx   <- label_start + c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4)                  * label_spacing  # 5 labels
strata_locx <- label_start + c(0, 0.03, 0.04, 0.05, 0.06, 0.09, 0.12, 0.13, 0.14, 0.15)
year_locx <- label_start + c(0, 0.02, 0.035, 0.05, 0.065, 0.08, 0.095, 0.11, 0.125) #8 labels
temp_locx   <- label_start + c(0, 1, 2)                        * label_spacing  # 3 labels


#gather all the plots
PA_structure<- ggdraw()+ theme_void()+
 draw_plot(plota_PA,x = 0, y = 0.25, width = 0.878, height = 0.75)+
 draw_plot(season,x = wx1, y= 0.2, width = wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(lake,x = wx1, y=0.15, width = wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(depth,x = wx1, y = 0.1, width =wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(YEAR,x = wx1, y = 0.05, width =wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(TEMP,x = wx1, y = 0, width =wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot_label(x = c(0.01,0.01,0.01,0.01,0.01), y = c(0.25,0.2,0.15,0.1,0.05), 
                     label =  c("Season","Lake","Depth","Year","Temp"),
                     hjust = 0, size = 7)+
  draw_plot_label(x=c(season_locx,lake_locx, strata_locx, year_locx, temp_locx), #temp
                  y=c(rep(0.25,2), #season x 2
                      rep(0.2,5), #lake x5
                      rep(0.15,10), #depth x 10
                      rep(0.1,9), #year x 9
                      rep(0.05,3)), #temp x3
                  label=c('Spring','Summer',
                          "SU","MI","HU","ER","ON",
                          names_depth,
                          "201","2","3","4","5","6","7","8","9",
                          'Low','High', 'NA'), 
                  color=c(season_colors["Spring"], season_colors["Summer"],
                          "#92809E", "#8cabea", "#64baaa","#FFC86A","#ee7061",
                          colors_depth,
                          "gray",'red','#A6CEE3','#B2DF8A',"brown","violet","purple","blue","black",
                          "blue","red","gray"),
                  hjust = 0, size = 6 )


  
  
ggsave("output/plots/PA/PA_sample_membership.pdf", PA_structure, width = 7, height = 3, units = "in",dpi=600)
  
PA_structure
```




### topic location
```{r}
#meta long
PA_meta_long<- melt.data.frame(subset(PA_meta,strat_status != "missing_data"), id.vars="Sample_num", variable_name="variable")
W_long_meta_PA<-full_join(PA_meta_long, W_long_PA, by = "Sample_num")

p_season<-subset(W_long_meta_PA,(variable == "SEASON") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(season_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=season_colors)+
      theme_bw()+
      labs(fill="Season",title="",y="Relative contribution",x= "Subcommunity")

p_season

p_lake<-subset(W_long_meta_PA,(variable == "lake") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(lake_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=lake_colors)+
      theme_bw()+
      labs(fill="Lake",title="",y="",x= "")
p_lake

p_layer<-subset(W_long_meta_PA,(variable == "new_depths_mdh2") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(depth2_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=depth2_colors)+
      theme_classic()+
      labs(fill="Layer",title="",y="",x= "")+
      theme(
        legend.position="right",  # Adjust if needed
        legend.key.size=unit(0.5, "cm"),  # Reduce legend key size
        legend.text=element_text(size=8),  # Smaller legend text
        legend.spacing.x=unit(0.2, "cm")  # Reduce spacing between legend items
      )



p_layer

p_year<-subset(W_long_meta_PA,(variable == "YEAR") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(year_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=year_colors)+
      theme_bw()+
      labs(fill="Year",title="",y="",x= "")

p_year

p_status<-subset(W_long_meta_PA,(variable == "strat_status2") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill= value.x))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=strat_colors)+
      labs(x="",y="", fill="Strat. Type")+
      theme_bw()

p_status

#sample prop - how much does this sc represent overall 
hold<-subset(W_long_meta_PA, variable == "YEAR")  #can be any variable 
prop<-hold %>% group_by(topic) %>% dplyr::summarise(prop=round((sum(value.y)/sum(hold$value.y))*100))

p_dist<-ggplot(prop, aes(x=topic, y=prop, fill=topic)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label=paste0(round(prop, 1), "%")), 
            vjust=-0.5, 
            size=3, 
            fontface="bold") +  
  scale_fill_brewer(palette="Paired") +
  labs(x="", y="% of all sample distribution") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)))+
  theme(legend.position="none", 
        axis.text.y=element_blank(), 
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
        theme(axis.title=element_text(size=8, face="bold")))


figure = ggpubr::ggarrange(p_season, p_status, p_layer, p_lake, p_year,p_dist, ncol = 3, nrow = 2)

figure<-ggpubr::annotate_figure(figure, 
                        top = ggpubr::text_grob("PA PROK: Topic Distribution across samples", color = "black", 
                                        face = "bold", size = 12))

ggsave("output/plots/PA/PA_membership.pdf", figure, width = 9, height = 5, units = "in",dpi=600)

figure

```


```{r}

p_lake<-subset(W_long_meta_PA,(variable == "lake") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(lake_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=lake_colors)+
      scale_alpha_manual(values = c("yes" = 1, "no" = 0.3), guide = "none") +
      theme_classic()+
      labs(fill="Lake",title=expression(bold("B") * " Meta Overview"), 
           y="Relative contribution",x= "Subcommunity")
p_lake

p_layer<-subset(W_long_meta_PA,(variable == "new_depths_mdh2") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(depth2_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=depth2_colors)+
      scale_alpha_manual(values = c("yes" = 1, "no" = 0.3), guide = "none") +
      theme_classic()+
      labs(fill="Layer",title="",y="Relative contribution",x= "Subcommunity")+
      theme(
        legend.position="right",  # Adjust if needed
        legend.key.size=unit(0.5, "cm"),  # Reduce legend key size
        legend.text=element_text(size=8),  # Smaller legend text
        legend.spacing.x=unit(0.2, "cm")  # Reduce spacing between legend items
      )

PA_meta_plt<- ggpubr::ggarrange(p_lake, p_layer, ncol = 2, nrow = 1)
PA_meta_plt
```

##prep meta
```{r}
prep_PA   <- prep_topic_meta(W_PA, PA_meta)
```

## Limma
```{r}
limma_res_PA_clr <- run_topic_clr_limma(prep_PA$W_clean, prep_PA$meta_clean)
sig_limma_clr_PA  <- limma_res_PA_clr$res_df  %>% filter(adj_p_value_by_term < 0.05)
sig_limma_clr_PA 
```
```{r}
PA_limma_crl <- plot_limma(
  sig_limma_clr_PA ,
  topic_order = colnames(prep_PA$W_clean),
  title       = expression(bold("C") * " Limma") ,
  meta_names  = meta_names,
  param_order = param_order
)

PA_limma_crl
```


## RM + SHAP
```{r}
results_clr_PA <- run_rf_oob_clr(prep_PA$W_clean, prep_PA$meta_clean)
results_clr_PA
```
```{r}
#shap_dir_PA <- run_shap_direction(
#  rf_models  = results_clr_PA$rf_models,
#  meta_clean = prep_PA$meta_clean,
#  nsim       = 500
#)

#save so we don't need to re-run everytime
saveRDS(shap_dir_PA, file = "output/objects/PA/shap_dir_PA.rds")
shap_dir_PA <- readRDS("output/objects/PA/shap_dir_PA.rds")

shap_dir_PA
```

```{r}
# get the new names in the same order as param_order
new_order <- meta_names$new[match(param_order, meta_names$original)]

shap_dir_PA2<- shap_dir_PA %>%
  left_join(meta_names, by = c("Env" = "original")) %>%
  mutate(Env= coalesce(new, Env)) %>% 
  mutate(Env = factor(Env, levels = new_order))

PA_rfshap<-plot_importance_direction_dot(
  shap_dir    = shap_dir_PA2,
  rf_perf     = results_clr_PA$rf_perf,
  top_n       = 15,
  param_order = new_order,
  title       = expression(bold("D") * " RM + SHAP")
)

#ggsave("../output/plots/FL/FL_RFshap.png", p, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in",dpi=600)

PA_rfshap
```

#Summary plot
```{r}
PA_limma_crl_tight <- PA_limma_crl + theme(plot.margin = margin(0, -2, 0, 0))
PA_rfshap_tight    <- PA_rfshap    + theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, -2))
bottom_right <- plot_grid(PA_limma_crl_tight, PA_rfshap_tight, ncol = 2,rel_widths = c(.8, 1.2))


right_col <- plot_grid(PA_meta_plt, bottom_right, nrow = 2,rel_heights = c(.8, 1.3))

#grab ht_PA as a grob if it's a ComplexHeatmap
ht_grob <- grid::grid.grabExpr(ComplexHeatmap::draw(ht_PA))
PA_summary <- plot_grid(ht_grob, right_col, 
                        ncol = 2,
                        align = "h",
                        axis = "tb",
                        rel_widths = c(1, 1.3))

ggsave("output/plots/PA/PA_summary.pdf", PA_summary, width = 18, height = 8, units = "in",dpi=600)
PA_summary
```

#____________
#### colors
```{r}
#colors
library(RColorBrewer)

# Get the 9-color Paired palette
paired_colors <- brewer.pal(8, "Paired")

# Create SSF colors
SSF_colors <- paired_colors
names(SSF_colors) <- paste0("SSF_", seq_along(SSF_colors))

# Create LSF colors 
my_palette <- c("#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd", 
                "#8c564b", "#e377c2", "#7f7f7f", "#bcbd22", "#17becf", 
                "#fdae61")


LSF_colors <- my_palette 
names(LSF_colors) <- paste0("LSF_", seq_along(LSF_colors))

# Combine into one named vector
chl_scs <- c(SSF_colors, LSF_colors)

chl_scs

```

#**SSF**

```{r}
## same processing as the proks but we need to change the taxonomy labels 
process_phyloseq_EUK <- function(ps) {
  library(phyloseq)
  
  # Extract the three tables
  if (taxa_are_rows(ps)) {
    asv <- as.data.frame(t(otu_table(ps)))
  } else {
    asv <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps))
  }
  
  tax  <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps))
  meta <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps))
  
  # Hand off to process_tables so we can clean if needed 
  return(process_tables_EUK(asv, tax, meta))
}

process_tables_EUK <- function(asv, tax, meta) {
  library(dplyr)
  
  tax$ASV <- rownames(tax)
  
  #we need asvs to be unique so we add a numer
  tax_dict <- tax %>%
    mutate(
      ASV_name = paste0(
        ifelse(is.na(Class), "[unknown Class]", Class),
        " (",
        ifelse(is.na(Order), "?", Order),
        ") ",
        row_number()
      )
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  tax_dict$ASV <- factor(tax_dict$ASV, levels = colnames(asv))
  tax_dict <- tax_dict[order(tax_dict$ASV), ]
  
  colnames(asv) <- tax_dict$ASV_name
  asv <- as.data.frame(asv) %>% mutate(across(everything(), as.integer))
  
  return(list(
    counts   = as.data.frame(asv),
    tax_dict = tax_dict,
    meta     = meta
  ))
}
```


#2. Prep Data
```{r}
#this inlcudes counts, tax_dict, meta
SSF_results<-process_phyloseq_EUK(SSF_data)

#save the counts tables you will need for perplexity
write.csv(SSF_results$counts, "output/tables/runSSF/SSF_counts.csv")

#free up some memoery we don't need the FL_data anymore and we can call it in if we do 
rm(SSF_data)
gc()  

```

#3. Run LDA
### run LDA models 
```{r run-lda-models}
lda_varying_params_lists_SSF <-  list()
for (k in 1:20) {lda_varying_params_lists_SSF[[paste0("k",k)]] <- list(k = k)}

lda_models_SSF  <- 
  run_lda_models(
    data = SSF_results$counts,
    lda_varying_params_lists = lda_varying_params_lists_SSF,
    lda_fixed_params_list = list(method = "VEM"),
    dir = "../output/saved_models/runsSSF/",
    reset = FALSE,
    verbose = TRUE
  )

```

### align the topics based on different methods
```{r align-topics}

aligned_topics_product_SSF <- 
  align_topics(
    models = lda_models_SSF,
    method = "product") 

compute_number_of_paths(aligned_topics_product_SSF) %>% 
  plot_number_of_paths() + 
  ggtitle("Method: product")

aligned_topics_transport_SSF <- 
  align_topics(
    models = lda_models_SSF,
    method = "transport") 

compute_number_of_paths(aligned_topics_transport_SSF) %>% 
  plot_number_of_paths() + 
  ggtitle("Method: transport")

```

### top taxa alingment
```{r}
aligned_topics_transport_k <- 
  align_topics(
    models = lda_models_SSF[c(1,10)],
    method = "transport") 

p<-plot(aligned_topics_transport_k,
     add_leaves = TRUE, 
     label_topics = TRUE,
     n_features_in_leaves = 2,
     min_feature_prop = 0.01,
     leaves_text_size = 8,
     color_by="topic",
      model_name_repair_fun = function(x) str_remove_all(x, "k"))+
     theme_bw()+
    labs(x="")

#ggsave(p, filename = "PA_k10.png", height = 5, width = 9, units = "cm", scale = 1.5)

p
```

### perplexity (for diagnostics)
```{r}
SSF_perplexity = read.csv("perplexity/SSF_perplexity.csv",header=TRUE)

p<-ggplot(SSF_perplexity, aes(x = k, y = perplexity)) +
   geom_point() +
   geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
   theme_bw()+
   labs(x = "Candidate number of topics", y = "Perplexity score")

p
```

##diagnostics figure
```{r}
figure <- make_figure2(aligned_topics_product = aligned_topics_product_SSF, 
                           aligned_topics_transport = aligned_topics_transport_SSF,
                       perplexity_df=SSF_perplexity,size_fraction="Small Chloroplast")


ggsave(figure, filename = "output/plots/SSF/diagnostics.png", height = 14, width = 21, units = "cm", scale = 1.5)

figure

```

Panel a: Paths track close to the diagonal at k=9, indicating a well-balanced model
Panel b: Coherence scores are mostly strong (0.5–0.8) at k=9, before degradation sets in at higher k
Panel c: Refinement score is near its peak at k=9, before variance becomes unstable
Panel d: k=9 sits just past the perplexity elbow, capturing most model improvement efficiently

### W and A
```{r}
W_SSF = as.data.frame(lda_models_SSF$k9$gamma)
colnames(W_SSF)<- sub("V", "", colnames(W_SSF))
W_SSF
```

```{r}
A_SSF <-as.data.frame(exp(lda_models_SSF$k9$beta))
A_df_SSF<-A_SSF ## to work on rare
A_SSF <- A_SSF %>% mutate(topic = row_number())

#change the ASV names 
A_long_SSF<- melt.data.frame(A_SSF, id.vars = "topic",variable_name="ASV")
A_long_SSF$Phyla<-sapply(strsplit(as.character(A_long_SSF$ASV)," "), "[", 1)
A_long_SSF$Order<-paste(sapply(strsplit(as.character(A_long_SSF$ASV)," "), "[", 2),
                    sapply(strsplit(as.character(A_long_SSF$ASV)," "), "[", 3),sep="_")
A_long_SSF$Order_raw<-paste(sapply(strsplit(as.character(A_long_SSF$Order),"_"), "[", 1))

#A_long_SSF<- A_long_SSF
#save(A_long_SSF,file="../output/tables/w_matrix/pa_tax")


A_long_SSF <- A_long_SSF %>% mutate(asv_type=case_when(value >= 0.1 ~ ">=10%", value < 0.1 ~ "<10%"))
A_long_SSF
```

#4.Run GoMDE
```{r}
res_SSF  <- run_gomde_markers(SSF_results$counts, SSF_results$tax_dict,W_SSF,A_long_SSF,  top_n = 25)

#save so you don't have to re-computer
saveRDS(res_SSF,"output/objects/SSF/res_SSF.rds")
res_SSF<-readRDS("output/objects/SSF/res_SSF.rds")

res_SSF
```

### overview
```{r}
#grab data from full set not top25 -- but still only SPECIALIST
mat_df <- res_SSF$gomde %>%
  filter(lfsr < 0.05, log2FC > 0) %>% #sig enrichment 
  group_by(ASV) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(topic) == 1) %>%      # specialists only
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(SSF_results$tax_dict, by = c("ASV" = "ASV_name")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Order)) %>%
  filter(!Order %in% c("unclass_plas")) %>%
  mutate(Class_clean = ifelse(Class %in% names(class_colors), Class, "Other")) %>%
  group_by(topic, Class, Class_clean, Order) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n_asv = n(), .groups = "drop")


mat_df

## collapse orders we only see in 1 topic
min_asv <- 5  # orders with max < 3 across all topics become Other [Phylum]

# build matrix
# build full matrix first to know which orders are sparse
mat_full <- mat_df %>%
  group_by(Order, topic) %>%
  summarise(n_asv = sum(n_asv), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = topic, values_from = n_asv, values_fill = 0) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("Order") %>%
  as.matrix()

# identify sparse orders
sparse_orders <- rownames(mat_full)[apply(mat_full, 1, max) < min_asv]

# collapse in mat_df
mat_df_collapsed <- mat_df %>%
  mutate(Order_plot = ifelse(Order %in% sparse_orders,
                           ifelse(Class_clean == "Other", 
                                  "Other (rare Class)",  # catch the Other Other case this is when we made phyla other
                                  paste0("Multiple ", Class_clean)),
                           Order)) %>% 
  group_by(topic, Class_clean, Order_plot) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n_asv = sum(n_asv), .groups = "drop")

# rebuild matrix
mat <- mat_df_collapsed %>%
  group_by(Order_plot, topic) %>%
  summarise(n_asv = sum(n_asv), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = topic, values_from = n_asv, values_fill = 0) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("Order_plot") %>%
  as.matrix()

mat <- mat[, order(as.numeric(colnames(mat)))]
colnames(mat) <- paste0("SSF_", colnames(mat))
mat <- mat[rowSums(mat) > 0, ]


order_class <- mat_df_collapsed %>%
  distinct(Order_plot, Class_clean) %>%
  filter(Order_plot %in% rownames(mat))

class_vec <- setNames(order_class$Class_clean, order_class$Order_plot)
class_vec <- class_vec[rownames(mat)]

class_colors_clean <- class_colors

# lighten phylum colors for Other rows
lighten <- function(hex, factor = 0.5) {
  rgb <- col2rgb(hex)
  rgb_light <- rgb + (255 - rgb) * factor
  rgb(rgb_light[1,], rgb_light[2,], rgb_light[3,], maxColorValue = 255)
}

# build annotation colors - named orders get full color, Other gets lightened
row_colors <- sapply(rownames(mat), function(ord) {
  class <- class_vec[ord]
  base_col <- class_colors_clean[class]
  if (grepl("^Multiple ", ord)) lighten(base_col, 0.5) else base_col
})

# for ComplexHeatmap we need the annotation to use the phylum color vector
# but mark Other rows differently with a border or pattern - simplest is
# just to show them as a named color per row
row_annot <- rowAnnotation(
  Class = class_vec,
  col = list(Class = class_colors_clean),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE,
  annotation_legend_param = list(Class = list(ncol = 1)),
  show_legend = TRUE  
)


# color scale
col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(0, max(mat)), c("white", "#2C7FB8"))

row_title <- rowAnnotation(
  label = anno_text("Order", 
                    rot = 90, 
                    just = "center",
                    gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold")),
  width = unit(0.8, "cm")
)

row_name_cols <- ifelse(grepl("^Multiple", rownames(mat)), "grey50", "black")
row_name_fontface <- ifelse(grepl("^Multiple ", rownames(mat)), "italic", "plain")

ht_SSF <- Heatmap(mat,
  name = "ASV count",
  col = col_fun,
  left_annotation = row_annot,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  show_row_dend = TRUE,
  show_heatmap_legend = FALSE,
  row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                      col = row_name_cols,
                      fontface = row_name_fontface),
  column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10),
  column_title = expression(bold("A") * " Fingerprint Taxa"),
  column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold"),
  rect_gp = gpar(col = "grey90", lwd = 0.5),
  width = unit(10, "cm"),
  row_names_max_width = max_text_width(rownames(mat), gp = gpar(fontsize = 8)),
  layer_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
    v <- pindex(mat, i, j)
    grid.text(ifelse(v > 0, as.character(v), ""), x, y,
              gp = gpar(fontsize = 7, col = "grey20"))
  },
  row_title = "Order",
  row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
  row_title_side = "right"
) 

ht_SSF

```


### top markers summary
```{r}
# swarm data FIRST (defines topic order)
df_swarm <- res_SSF$top_markers %>%
  group_by(ASV) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(topic) == 1) %>%        # pecialists only
  ungroup() %>%
   mutate(
    topic  = factor(topic),
    Class = ifelse(Class %in% names(class_colors), Class, "Other")
  )

# pie composition per topic
pie_df <- df_swarm %>%
  count(topic, Class, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(topic) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(topic, Class, prop) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Class, values_from = prop, values_fill = 0)

# CONTROL PIE LOCATION (global annotation band)
topic_levels <- levels(df_swarm$topic)
pie_df <- pie_df %>%
  mutate(x = as.numeric(factor(topic, levels = topic_levels)))

y_max <- max(df_swarm$log2FC, na.rm = TRUE)
pie_df$y <- y_max + 1.5   # <-- you can set this to whatever you want

pie_cols <- intersect(names(pie_df), names(class_colors))

## topic levels
topic_levels <- df_swarm %>%
  distinct(topic) %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(sub(".*_", "", topic))) %>%
  pull(topic)

df_swarm$topic <- factor(df_swarm$topic, levels = topic_levels)
pie_df$topic  <- factor(pie_df$topic,  levels = topic_levels)
pie_df$x <- as.numeric(pie_df$topic)


p_swarm_pie_SSF <- ggplot(df_swarm, aes(x = topic, y = log2FC, color = Class)) + #size = log2FC
  geom_quasirandom(width = 0.3, alpha = .8,stroke = 0.9 ) +
  scatterpie::geom_scatterpie(
    data = pie_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    cols = pie_cols,
    pie_scale = 3,
    color = "white",
    linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = class_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = class_colors) +
  guides(fill = "none", color = guide_legend(ncol = 4,byrow = TRUE)) + #size = guide_legend(ncol = 1)
  #guides(fill = "none")+
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Topic", y = "GoM-DE log2FC - Top25", color = "Class")+ #size = "log2FC"
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.6, ylim = c(0, y_max + 2))+
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(angle = 90),
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.1, "mm"),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.1, "mm"),
     legend.key.size = unit(1, "mm"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal")

p_swarm_pie_SSF
```

```{r}
asv_topic_sig_SSF <- res_SSF$gomde %>%
  filter(lfsr < 0.05, log2FC > 0) %>%            # enriched only
  left_join(distinct(SSF_results$tax_dict, ASV_name, Class),
            by = c("ASV" = "ASV_name")) %>%
  mutate(
    Class = ifelse(Class %in% names(class_colors), Class, "Other"),
    Class = factor(Class, levels = names(class_colors))
  ) %>%
  group_by(ASV) %>%
  summarise(
    Class = dplyr::first(Class),
    n_topics = n_distinct(topic),
    max_log2FC = max(log2FC),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

df_hm_SSF <- asv_topic_sig_SSF %>%
  count(Class, n_topics)
print(sum(df_hm_SSF$n))

pheat_SSF<-ggplot(df_hm_SSF, aes(x = factor(n_topics), y = Class, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#f2e5ff",
    high = "#5b2a86"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of topics where ASV is significantly enriched",
    y = "Class",
    fill = "ASV count",
    title = paste0(
      "Distribution of marker ASVs (n=",
      nrow(asv_topic_sig_SSF),
      ")"
     )
  )
pheat_SSF
```

```{r}
figure <-  ggpubr::ggarrange(pheat_SSF,p_swarm_pie_SSF, ncol = 2, nrow = 1,widths = c(.4,.6))

figure<-annotate_figure(figure,
                top = text_grob("Small Euks", color = "black", face = "bold", size = 14))

ggsave(file="output/plots/SSF/gomde_SSF_pie.pdf", figure, width = 12, height = 8, units = "in",dpi=600)

figure
```

#5.Run Env Analysis

### topic proportion across samples
```{r}
## cluster by similarity
w_den<-as.dendrogram(as.hclust(agnes(W_SSF, diss=F, method =  "ward")),hang=.5)

#data based on the clustering above 
W_SSF2<-W_SSF
W_SSF2$Sample_num<-row.names(W_SSF2)
W_long_SSF<- melt.data.frame( W_SSF2, id.vars="Sample_num", variable_name="topic")

plota_SSF<-W_long_SSF %>%
      ggplot(aes(x=factor(Sample_num,level=labels(w_den)), value, fill=topic))+
      geom_col(color=NA,width=1,linewidth=0)+
      scale_fill_brewer(palette="Paired")+
      labs(y="Subcommunity Proportion",x=paste0("Samples (n=", dim(W_SSF2)[1],")"),fill="SC#",
           title=expression(bold("C") * " SSF Chloroplast"))+
      theme_bw()+
    scale_x_discrete(labels = NULL, breaks = NULL) + 
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),expand = c(0, 0))+
      theme(axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
             plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
          legend.key.height= unit(.05, 'in'),
        legend.key.width= unit(.05, 'in'),
        legend.box.spacing = unit(0.01, "pt"))

ggsave("output/plots/SSF/SSF_structure.pdf", plota_SSF, width = 7, height = 2.5, units = "in",dpi=600)

plota_SSF

```

```{r}
SSF_meta<-data.frame(SSF_results$meta, check.names = FALSE)

#PLOTS B-D: legend bars
#get sample infor from main clean table
sample_info<-SSF_meta[,c('Sample_num','new_depths_mdh2','CTD_TEMP','SEASON','YEAR','lake')]
#drop duplicates - because each OTU gets a value so we have many reps 
colnames(sample_info)<-c('sample_id','depth','CTD_TEMP','SEASON','YEAR','lake')
#reorder to match the order of the bar plots 
sample_info<-sample_info %>% arrange(sample_id,levels=labels(w_den))
sample_info$sample_id<- factor(sample_info$sample_id,level=labels(w_den))

###make the bar plots 
lake<-ggplot(data=sample_info, aes(x=sample_id, fill=lake)) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values=lake_colors)+
  geom_bar(width = 1,linewidth=0)+ theme_void()+
  theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
        axis.text.y=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position="none")+xlab("") + ylab("")

season<-ggplot(data=sample_info, aes(x=sample_id, fill=SEASON)) + 
  geom_bar(width = 1,linewidth=0)+ theme_void()+
  scale_fill_manual(values=season_colors)+
  theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
        axis.text.y=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position="none")+xlab("") + ylab("")

TEMP<-ggplot(data=sample_info, aes(x=sample_id, fill=CTD_TEMP)) + 
geom_bar(width = 1,linewidth=0)+ theme_void()+
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red")+
theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
      axis.text.y=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
  strip.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position="none")+xlab("") + ylab("")
  
YEAR<-ggplot(data=sample_info, aes(x=sample_id, fill=factor(YEAR))) + 
geom_bar(width = 1,linewidth=0)+ theme_void()+
  scale_fill_manual(values=year_colors)+
theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
      axis.text.y=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
  strip.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position="none")+xlab("") + ylab("")

depth<-ggplot(data=sample_info, aes(x=sample_id, fill=depth)) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values=depth_colors_w)+
  geom_bar(width = 1,size=0)+ theme_void()+
  theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
        axis.text.y=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position="none")+xlab("") + ylab("")

wx1=0.072
wx2=0.73

### value definitions
names_depth=c("spr:","I","E","T","H","smr:","E","D","T","H")

colors_depth= c(season_colors["Spring"],depth_colors_w["Iso_Top"], depth_colors_w["InvS_EPI"],
          depth_colors_w["InvS_TRM"], depth_colors_w["InvS_HYP"], season_colors["Summer"],
          depth_colors_w["Strat_EPI"],depth_colors_w["Strat_DCL"],depth_colors_w["Strat_TRM"],
          depth_colors_w["Strat_HYP"])

#change dynamically
label_start   <- wx1 + wx2 + 0.01
label_spacing <- 0.03

season_locx <- label_start + c(0, 2)                            * label_spacing  # 2 labels
lake_locx   <- label_start + c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4)                  * label_spacing  # 5 labels
strata_locx <- label_start + c(0, 0.03, 0.04, 0.05, 0.06, 0.09, 0.12, 0.13, 0.14, 0.15)
year_locx <- label_start + c(0, 0.02, 0.035, 0.05, 0.065, 0.08, 0.095, 0.11, 0.125) #8 labels
temp_locx   <- label_start + c(0, 1, 2)                        * label_spacing  # 3 labels


#gather all the plots
SSF_structure<- ggdraw()+ theme_void()+
 draw_plot(plota_SSF,x = 0, y = 0.25, width = 0.878, height = 0.75)+
 draw_plot(season,x = wx1, y= 0.2, width = wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(lake,x = wx1, y=0.15, width = wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(depth,x = wx1, y = 0.1, width =wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(YEAR,x = wx1, y = 0.05, width =wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(TEMP,x = wx1, y = 0, width =wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot_label(x = c(0.01,0.01,0.01,0.01,0.01), y = c(0.25,0.2,0.15,0.1,0.05), 
                     label =  c("Season","Lake","Depth","Year","Temp"),
                     hjust = 0, size = 7)+
  draw_plot_label(x=c(season_locx,lake_locx, strata_locx, year_locx, temp_locx), #temp
                  y=c(rep(0.25,2), #season x 2
                      rep(0.2,5), #lake x5
                      rep(0.15,10), #depth x 10
                      rep(0.1,9), #year x 9
                      rep(0.05,3)), #temp x3
                  label=c('Spring','Summer',
                          "SU","MI","HU","ER","ON",
                          names_depth,
                          "201","2","3","4","5","6","7","8","9",
                          'Low','High', 'NA'), 
                  color=c(season_colors["Spring"], season_colors["Summer"],
                          "#92809E", "#8cabea", "#64baaa","#FFC86A","#ee7061",
                          colors_depth,
                          "gray",'red','#A6CEE3','#B2DF8A',"brown","violet","purple","blue","black",
                          "blue","red","gray"),
                  hjust = 0, size = 6 )


  
  
ggsave("output/plots/SSF/SSF_sample_membership.pdf", SSF_structure, width = 7, height = 3, units = "in",dpi=600)
  
SSF_structure
```




### topic location
```{r}
#meta long
SSF_meta_long<- melt.data.frame(subset(SSF_meta,strat_status != "missing_data"), id.vars="Sample_num", variable_name="variable")
W_long_meta_SSF<-full_join(SSF_meta_long, W_long_SSF, by = "Sample_num")

p_season<-subset(W_long_meta_SSF,(variable == "SEASON") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(season_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=season_colors)+
      theme_bw()+
      labs(fill="Season",title="",y="Relative contribution",x= "Subcommunity")

p_season

p_lake<-subset(W_long_meta_SSF,(variable == "lake") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(lake_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=lake_colors)+
      theme_bw()+
      labs(fill="Lake",title="",y="",x= "")
p_lake

p_layer<-subset(W_long_meta_SSF,(variable == "new_depths_mdh2") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(depth2_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=depth2_colors)+
      theme_classic()+
      labs(fill="Layer",title="",y="",x= "")+
      theme(
        legend.position="right",  # Adjust if needed
        legend.key.size=unit(0.5, "cm"),  # Reduce legend key size
        legend.text=element_text(size=8),  # Smaller legend text
        legend.spacing.x=unit(0.2, "cm")  # Reduce spacing between legend items
      )



p_layer

p_year<-subset(W_long_meta_SSF,(variable == "YEAR") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(year_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=year_colors)+
      theme_bw()+
      labs(fill="Year",title="",y="",x= "")

p_year

p_status<-subset(W_long_meta_SSF,(variable == "strat_status2") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill= value.x))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=strat_colors)+
      labs(x="",y="", fill="Strat. Type")+
      theme_bw()

p_status

#sample prop - how much does this sc represent overall 
hold<-subset(W_long_meta_SSF, variable == "YEAR")  #can be any variable 
prop<-hold %>% group_by(topic) %>% dplyr::summarise(prop=round((sum(value.y)/sum(hold$value.y))*100))

p_dist<-ggplot(prop, aes(x=topic, y=prop, fill=topic)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label=paste0(round(prop, 1), "%")), 
            vjust=-0.5, 
            size=3, 
            fontface="bold") +  
  scale_fill_brewer(palette="Paired") +
  labs(x="", y="% of all sample distribution") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)))+
  theme(legend.position="none", 
        axis.text.y=element_blank(), 
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
        theme(axis.title=element_text(size=8, face="bold")))


figure = ggpubr::ggarrange(p_season, p_status, p_layer, p_lake, p_year,p_dist, ncol = 3, nrow = 2)

figure<-ggpubr::annotate_figure(figure, 
                        top = ggpubr::text_grob("SSF EUK: Topic Distribution across samples", color = "black", 
                                        face = "bold", size = 12))

ggsave("output/plots/SSF/SSF_membership.pdf", figure, width = 9, height = 5, units = "in",dpi=600)

figure

```


```{r}

p_lake<-subset(W_long_meta_SSF,(variable == "lake") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(lake_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=lake_colors)+
      scale_alpha_manual(values = c("yes" = 1, "no" = 0.3), guide = "none") +
      theme_classic()+
      labs(fill="Lake",title=expression(bold("B") * " Meta Overview"), 
           y="Relative contribution",x= "Subcommunity")
p_lake

p_layer<-subset(W_long_meta_SSF,(variable == "new_depths_mdh2") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(depth2_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=depth2_colors)+
      scale_alpha_manual(values = c("yes" = 1, "no" = 0.3), guide = "none") +
      theme_classic()+
      labs(fill="Layer",title="",y="Relative contribution",x= "Subcommunity")+
      theme(
        legend.position="right",  # Adjust if needed
        legend.key.size=unit(0.5, "cm"),  # Reduce legend key size
        legend.text=element_text(size=8),  # Smaller legend text
        legend.spacing.x=unit(0.2, "cm")  # Reduce spacing between legend items
      )

SSF_meta_plt<- ggpubr::ggarrange(p_lake, p_layer, ncol = 2, nrow = 1)
SSF_meta_plt
```

##prep meta
```{r}
prep_SSF   <- prep_topic_meta(W_SSF, SSF_meta)
```
## Limma
```{r}
limma_res_SSF_clr <- run_topic_clr_limma(prep_SSF$W_clean, prep_SSF$meta_clean)
sig_limma_clr_SSF  <- limma_res_SSF_clr$res_df  %>% filter(adj_p_value_by_term < 0.05)
sig_limma_clr_SSF 
```

```{r}
SSF_limma_crl <- plot_limma(
  sig_limma_clr_SSF ,
  topic_order = colnames(prep_SSF$W_clean),
  title       = expression(bold("C") * " Limma") ,
  meta_names  = meta_names,
  param_order = param_order
)

SSF_limma_crl
```


## RM + SHAP
```{r}
results_clr_SSF <- run_rf_oob_clr(prep_SSF$W_clean, prep_SSF$meta_clean)
results_clr_SSF
```
```{r}
shap_dir_SSF <- run_shap_direction(
  rf_models  = results_clr_SSF$rf_models,
  meta_clean = prep_SSF$meta_clean,
  nsim       = 500
)

#save so we don't need to re-run everytime
saveRDS(shap_dir_SSF, file = "output/objects/SSF/shap_dir_SSF.rds")
shap_dir_SSF <- readRDS("output/objects/SSF/shap_dir_SSF.rds")

shap_dir_SSF
```

```{r}
# get the new names in the same order as param_order
new_order <- meta_names$new[match(param_order, meta_names$original)]

shap_dir_SSF2<- shap_dir_SSF %>%
  left_join(meta_names, by = c("Env" = "original")) %>%
  mutate(Env= coalesce(new, Env)) %>% 
  mutate(Env = factor(Env, levels = new_order))

SSF_rfshap<-plot_importance_direction_dot(
  shap_dir    = shap_dir_SSF2,
  rf_perf     = subset(results_clr_SSF$rf_perf, Topic !="6"), ##6 is dropped from Limma so drop here too it has low R2
  top_n       = 15,
  param_order = new_order,
  title       = expression(bold("D") * " RM + SHAP")
)

#ggsave("../output/plots/FL/FL_RFshap.png", p, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in",dpi=600)

SSF_rfshap

```

#Summary plot
```{r}
SSF_limma_crl_tight <- SSF_limma_crl + theme(plot.margin = margin(0, -2, 0, 0))
SSF_rfshap_tight    <- SSF_rfshap    + theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, -2))
bottom_right <- plot_grid(SSF_limma_crl_tight, SSF_rfshap_tight, ncol = 2,rel_widths = c(.8, 1.2))


right_col <- plot_grid(SSF_meta_plt, bottom_right, nrow = 2,rel_heights = c(.8, 1.3))

#grab ht_SSF as a grob if it's a ComplexHeatmap
ht_grob <- grid::grid.grabExpr(ComplexHeatmap::draw(ht_SSF))
SSF_summary <- plot_grid(ht_grob, right_col, 
                        ncol = 2,
                        align = "h",
                        axis = "tb",
                        rel_widths = c(1, 1.3))

ggsave("output/plots/SSF/SSF_summary.pdf", SSF_summary, width = 18, height = 8, units = "in",dpi=600)
SSF_summary
```



#____________
#**LSF**

#2. Prep Data
```{r}
#this inlcudes counts, tax_dict, meta
LSF_results<-process_phyloseq_EUK(LSF_data)

#save the counts tables you will need for perplexity
write.csv(LSF_results$counts, "output/tables/runLSF/LSF_counts.csv")

#free up some memoery we don't need the FL_data anymore and we can call it in if we do 
rm(LSF_data)
gc()  

```

#3. Run LDA
### run LDA models 
```{r run-lda-models}
lda_varying_params_lists_LSF <-  list()
for (k in 1:20) {lda_varying_params_lists_LSF[[paste0("k",k)]] <- list(k = k)}

lda_models_LSF  <- 
  run_lda_models(
    data = LSF_results$counts,
    lda_varying_params_lists = lda_varying_params_lists_LSF,
    lda_fixed_params_list = list(method = "VEM"),
    dir = "../output/saved_models/runsLSF/",
    #reset = TRUE,
    #verbose = TRUE
  )

```


### align the topics based on different methods
```{r align-topics}

aligned_topics_product_LSF <- 
  align_topics(
    models = lda_models_LSF,
    method = "product") 

compute_number_of_paths(aligned_topics_product_LSF) %>% 
  plot_number_of_paths() + 
  ggtitle("Method: product")

aligned_topics_transport_LSF <- 
  align_topics(
    models = lda_models_LSF,
    method = "transport") 

compute_number_of_paths(aligned_topics_transport_LSF) %>% 
  plot_number_of_paths() + 
  ggtitle("Method: transport")

```

### top taxa alingment
```{r}
aligned_topics_transport_k <- 
  align_topics(
    models = lda_models_LSF[c(1,13)],
    method = "transport") 

p<-plot(aligned_topics_transport_k,
     add_leaves = TRUE, 
     label_topics = TRUE,
     n_features_in_leaves = 2,
     min_feature_prop = 0.01,
     leaves_text_size = 8,
     color_by="topic",
      model_name_repair_fun = function(x) str_remove_all(x, "k"))+
     theme_bw()+
    labs(x="")

#ggsave(p, filename = "PA_k10.png", height = 5, width = 9, units = "cm", scale = 1.5)

p
```

### perplexity (for diagnostics)
```{r}
LSF_perplexity = read.csv("perplexity/LSF_perplexity.csv",header=TRUE)

p<-ggplot(LSF_perplexity, aes(x = k, y = perplexity)) +
   geom_point() +
   geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
   theme_bw()+
   labs(x = "Candidate number of topics", y = "Perplexity score")

p
```

##diagnostics figure
```{r}
figure <- make_figure2(aligned_topics_product = aligned_topics_product_LSF, 
                           aligned_topics_transport = aligned_topics_transport_LSF,
                       perplexity_df=LSF_perplexity,size_fraction="Large Chloroplast")


ggsave(figure, filename = "output/plots/LSF/diagnostics.png", height = 14, width = 21, units = "cm", scale = 1.5)

figure

```

Panel a: Paths track close to the diagonal at k=11
Panel b: Coherence scores are mostly strong (0.5–0.8) at k=11, before degradation sets in at higher k
Panel c: Refinement score has plateaued by k=11, with little gain from adding more topics
Panel d: k=11 sits at the flattening point of the perplexity curve, capturing most model improvement efficiently
And for interpretation less is better 


### W and A
```{r}
W_LSF = as.data.frame(lda_models_LSF$k11$gamma)
colnames(W_LSF)<- sub("V", "", colnames(W_LSF))
W_LSF
```

```{r}
A_LSF <-as.data.frame(exp(lda_models_LSF$k11$beta))
A_df_LSF<-A_LSF ## to work on rare
A_LSF <- A_LSF %>% mutate(topic = row_number())

#change the ASV names 
A_long_LSF<- melt.data.frame(A_LSF, id.vars = "topic",variable_name="ASV")
A_long_LSF$Phyla<-sapply(strsplit(as.character(A_long_LSF$ASV)," "), "[", 1)
A_long_LSF$Order<-paste(sapply(strsplit(as.character(A_long_LSF$ASV)," "), "[", 2),
                    sapply(strsplit(as.character(A_long_LSF$ASV)," "), "[", 3),sep="_")
A_long_LSF$Order_raw<-paste(sapply(strsplit(as.character(A_long_LSF$Order),"_"), "[", 1))

#A_long_LSF<- A_long_LSF
#save(A_long_LSF,file="../output/tables/w_matrix/pa_tax")


A_long_LSF <- A_long_LSF %>% mutate(asv_type=case_when(value >= 0.1 ~ ">=10%", value < 0.1 ~ "<10%"))
A_long_LSF
```

#4.Run GoMDE
```{r}
#res_LSF  <- run_gomde_markers(LSF_results$counts, LSF_results$tax_dict,W_LSF,A_long_LSF,  top_n = 25)

#save so you don't have to re-computer
#saveRDS(res_LSF,"output/objects/LSF/res_LSF.rds")
res_LSF<-readRDS("output/objects/LSF/res_LSF.rds")

res_LSF
```

### overview
```{r}
#grab data from full set not top25 -- but still only SPECIALIST
mat_df <- res_LSF$gomde %>%
  filter(lfsr < 0.05, log2FC > 0) %>% #sig enrichment 
  group_by(ASV) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(topic) == 1) %>%      # specialists only
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(LSF_results$tax_dict, by = c("ASV" = "ASV_name")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Order)) %>%
  filter(!Order %in% c("unclass_plas")) %>%
  mutate(Class_clean = ifelse(Class %in% names(class_colors), Class, "Other")) %>%
  group_by(topic, Class, Class_clean, Order) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n_asv = n(), .groups = "drop")


mat_df

## collapse orders we only see in 1 topic
min_asv <- 5  # orders with max < 3 across all topics become Other [Phylum]

# build matrix
# build full matrix first to know which orders are sparse
mat_full <- mat_df %>%
  group_by(Order, topic) %>%
  summarise(n_asv = sum(n_asv), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = topic, values_from = n_asv, values_fill = 0) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("Order") %>%
  as.matrix()

# identify sparse orders
sparse_orders <- rownames(mat_full)[apply(mat_full, 1, max) < min_asv]

# collapse in mat_df
mat_df_collapsed <- mat_df %>%
  mutate(Order_plot = ifelse(Order %in% sparse_orders,
                           ifelse(Class_clean == "Other", 
                                  "Other (rare Class)",  # catch the Other Other case this is when we made phyla other
                                  paste0("Multiple ", Class_clean)),
                           Order)) %>% 
  group_by(topic, Class_clean, Order_plot) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n_asv = sum(n_asv), .groups = "drop")

# rebuild matrix
mat <- mat_df_collapsed %>%
  group_by(Order_plot, topic) %>%
  summarise(n_asv = sum(n_asv), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = topic, values_from = n_asv, values_fill = 0) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("Order_plot") %>%
  as.matrix()

mat <- mat[, order(as.numeric(colnames(mat)))]
colnames(mat) <- paste0("LSF_", colnames(mat))
mat <- mat[rowSums(mat) > 0, ]


order_class <- mat_df_collapsed %>%
  distinct(Order_plot, Class_clean) %>%
  filter(Order_plot %in% rownames(mat))

class_vec <- setNames(order_class$Class_clean, order_class$Order_plot)
class_vec <- class_vec[rownames(mat)]

class_colors_clean <- class_colors

# lighten phylum colors for Other rows
lighten <- function(hex, factor = 0.5) {
  rgb <- col2rgb(hex)
  rgb_light <- rgb + (255 - rgb) * factor
  rgb(rgb_light[1,], rgb_light[2,], rgb_light[3,], maxColorValue = 255)
}

# build annotation colors - named orders get full color, Other gets lightened
row_colors <- sapply(rownames(mat), function(ord) {
  class <- class_vec[ord]
  base_col <- class_colors_clean[class]
  if (grepl("^Multiple ", ord)) lighten(base_col, 0.5) else base_col
})

# for ComplexHeatmap we need the annotation to use the phylum color vector
# but mark Other rows differently with a border or pattern - simplest is
# just to show them as a named color per row
row_annot <- rowAnnotation(
  Class = class_vec,
  col = list(Class = class_colors_clean),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE,
  annotation_legend_param = list(Class = list(ncol = 1)),
  show_legend = TRUE  
)


# color scale
col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(0, max(mat)), c("white", "#2C7FB8"))

row_title <- rowAnnotation(
  label = anno_text("Order", 
                    rot = 90, 
                    just = "center",
                    gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold")),
  width = unit(0.8, "cm")
)

row_name_cols <- ifelse(grepl("^Multiple", rownames(mat)), "grey50", "black")
row_name_fontface <- ifelse(grepl("^Multiple ", rownames(mat)), "italic", "plain")

ht_LSF <- Heatmap(mat,
  name = "ASV count",
  col = col_fun,
  left_annotation = row_annot,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  show_row_dend = TRUE,
  show_heatmap_legend = FALSE,
  row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                      col = row_name_cols,
                      fontface = row_name_fontface),
  column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10),
  column_title = expression(bold("A") * " Fingerprint Taxa"),
  column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold"),
  rect_gp = gpar(col = "grey90", lwd = 0.5),
  width = unit(10, "cm"),
  row_names_max_width = max_text_width(rownames(mat), gp = gpar(fontsize = 8)),
  layer_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
    v <- pindex(mat, i, j)
    grid.text(ifelse(v > 0, as.character(v), ""), x, y,
              gp = gpar(fontsize = 7, col = "grey20"))
  },
  row_title = "Order",
  row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
  row_title_side = "right"
) 

ht_LSF

```

## top markers summary
```{r}
# swarm data FIRST (defines topic order)
df_swarm <- res_LSF$top_markers %>%
  group_by(ASV) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(topic) == 1) %>%        # specialists only
  ungroup() %>%
   mutate(
    topic  = factor(topic),
    Class = ifelse(Class %in% names(class_colors), Class, "Other")
  )

# pie composition per topic
pie_df <- df_swarm %>%
  count(topic, Class, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(topic) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(topic, Class, prop) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Class, values_from = prop, values_fill = 0)

# CONTROL PIE LOCATION (global annotation band)
topic_levels <- levels(df_swarm$topic)
pie_df <- pie_df %>%
  mutate(x = as.numeric(factor(topic, levels = topic_levels)))

y_max <- max(df_swarm$log2FC, na.rm = TRUE)
pie_df$y <- y_max + 1.5   # <-- you can set this to whatever you want

pie_cols <- intersect(names(pie_df), names(class_colors))

## topic levels
topic_levels <- df_swarm %>%
  distinct(topic) %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(sub(".*_", "", topic))) %>%
  pull(topic)

df_swarm$topic <- factor(df_swarm$topic, levels = topic_levels)
pie_df$topic  <- factor(pie_df$topic,  levels = topic_levels)
pie_df$x <- as.numeric(pie_df$topic)


p_swarm_pie_LSF <- ggplot(df_swarm, aes(x = topic, y = log2FC, color = Class)) + #size = log2FC
  geom_quasirandom(width = 0.3, alpha = .8,stroke = 0.9 ) +
  scatterpie::geom_scatterpie(
    data = pie_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    cols = pie_cols,
    pie_scale = 3,
    color = "white",
    linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = class_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = class_colors) +
  guides(fill = "none", color = guide_legend(ncol = 4,byrow = TRUE)) + #size = guide_legend(ncol = 1)
  #guides(fill = "none")+
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Topic", y = "GoM-DE log2FC - Top25", color = "Class")+ #size = "log2FC"
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.6, ylim = c(0, y_max + 2))+
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(angle = 90),
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.1, "mm"),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.1, "mm"),
     legend.key.size = unit(1, "mm"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal")

p_swarm_pie_LSF
```

```{r}
asv_topic_sig_LSF <- res_LSF$gomde %>%
  filter(lfsr < 0.05, log2FC > 0) %>%            # enriched only
  left_join(distinct(LSF_results$tax_dict, ASV_name, Class),
            by = c("ASV" = "ASV_name")) %>%
  mutate(
    Class = ifelse(Class %in% names(class_colors), Class, "Other"),
    Class = factor(Class, levels = names(class_colors))
  ) %>%
  group_by(ASV) %>%
  summarise(
    Class = dplyr::first(Class),
    n_topics = n_distinct(topic),
    max_log2FC = max(log2FC),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

df_hm_LSF <- asv_topic_sig_LSF %>%
  count(Class, n_topics)
print(sum(df_hm_LSF$n))

pheat_LSF<-ggplot(df_hm_LSF, aes(x = factor(n_topics), y = Class, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#f2e5ff",
    high = "#5b2a86"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of topics where ASV is significantly enriched",
    y = "Class",
    fill = "ASV count",
    title = paste0(
      "Distribution of marker ASVs (n=",
      nrow(asv_topic_sig_LSF),
      ")"
     )
  )
pheat_LSF
```

```{r}
figure <-  ggpubr::ggarrange(pheat_LSF,p_swarm_pie_LSF, ncol = 2, nrow = 1,widths = c(.4,.6))

figure<-annotate_figure(figure,
                top = text_grob("Large Euks", color = "black", face = "bold", size = 14))

ggsave(file="output/plots/LSF/gomde_LSF_pie.pdf", figure, width = 12, height = 8, units = "in",dpi=600)

figure
```

#5.Run Env Analysis

### topic proportion across samples
```{r}
## cluster by similarity
w_den<-as.dendrogram(as.hclust(agnes(W_LSF, diss=F, method =  "ward")),hang=.5)

#data based on the clustering above 
W_LSF2<-W_LSF
W_LSF2$Sample_num<-row.names(W_LSF2)
W_long_LSF<- melt.data.frame( W_LSF2, id.vars="Sample_num", variable_name="topic")

plota_LSF<-W_long_LSF %>%
      ggplot(aes(x=factor(Sample_num,level=labels(w_den)), value, fill=topic))+
      geom_col(color=NA,width=1,linewidth=0)+
      scale_fill_brewer(palette="Paired")+
      labs(y="Subcommunity Proportion",x=paste0("Samples (n=", dim(W_LSF2)[1],")"),fill="SC#",
           title=expression(bold("D") * " LSF Chloroplast"))+
      theme_bw()+
    scale_x_discrete(labels = NULL, breaks = NULL) + 
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),expand = c(0, 0))+
      theme(axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
             plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
          legend.key.height= unit(.05, 'in'),
        legend.key.width= unit(.05, 'in'),
        legend.box.spacing = unit(0.01, "pt"))

ggsave("output/plots/LSF/LSF_structure.pdf", plota_LSF, width = 7, height = 2.5, units = "in",dpi=600)

plota_LSF

```

```{r}
LSF_meta<-data.frame(LSF_results$meta, check.names = FALSE)

#PLOTS B-D: legend bars
#get sample infor from main clean table
sample_info<-LSF_meta[,c('Sample_num','new_depths_mdh2','CTD_TEMP','SEASON','YEAR','lake')]
#drop duplicates - because each OTU gets a value so we have many reps 
colnames(sample_info)<-c('sample_id','depth','CTD_TEMP','SEASON','YEAR','lake')
#reorder to match the order of the bar plots 
sample_info<-sample_info %>% arrange(sample_id,levels=labels(w_den))
sample_info$sample_id<- factor(sample_info$sample_id,level=labels(w_den))

###make the bar plots 
lake<-ggplot(data=sample_info, aes(x=sample_id, fill=lake)) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values=lake_colors)+
  geom_bar(width = 1,linewidth=0)+ theme_void()+
  theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
        axis.text.y=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position="none")+xlab("") + ylab("")

season<-ggplot(data=sample_info, aes(x=sample_id, fill=SEASON)) + 
  geom_bar(width = 1,linewidth=0)+ theme_void()+
  scale_fill_manual(values=season_colors)+
  theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
        axis.text.y=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position="none")+xlab("") + ylab("")

TEMP<-ggplot(data=sample_info, aes(x=sample_id, fill=CTD_TEMP)) + 
geom_bar(width = 1,linewidth=0)+ theme_void()+
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red")+
theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
      axis.text.y=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
  strip.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position="none")+xlab("") + ylab("")
  
YEAR<-ggplot(data=sample_info, aes(x=sample_id, fill=factor(YEAR))) + 
geom_bar(width = 1,linewidth=0)+ theme_void()+
  scale_fill_manual(values=year_colors)+
theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
      axis.text.y=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
  strip.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position="none")+xlab("") + ylab("")

depth<-ggplot(data=sample_info, aes(x=sample_id, fill=depth)) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values=depth_colors_w)+
  geom_bar(width = 1,size=0)+ theme_void()+
  theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
        axis.text.y=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position="none")+xlab("") + ylab("")

wx1=0.072
wx2=0.73

### value definitions
names_depth=c("spr:","I","E","T","H","smr:","E","D","T","H")

colors_depth= c(season_colors["Spring"],depth_colors_w["Iso_Top"], depth_colors_w["InvS_EPI"],
          depth_colors_w["InvS_TRM"], depth_colors_w["InvS_HYP"], season_colors["Summer"],
          depth_colors_w["Strat_EPI"],depth_colors_w["Strat_DCL"],depth_colors_w["Strat_TRM"],
          depth_colors_w["Strat_HYP"])

#change dynamically
label_start   <- wx1 + wx2 + 0.01
label_spacing <- 0.03

season_locx <- label_start + c(0, 2)                            * label_spacing  # 2 labels
lake_locx   <- label_start + c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4)                  * label_spacing  # 5 labels
strata_locx <- label_start + c(0, 0.03, 0.04, 0.05, 0.06, 0.09, 0.12, 0.13, 0.14, 0.15)
year_locx <- label_start + c(0, 0.02, 0.035, 0.05, 0.065, 0.08, 0.095, 0.11, 0.125) #8 labels
temp_locx   <- label_start + c(0, 1, 2)                        * label_spacing  # 3 labels


#gather all the plots
LSF_structure<- ggdraw()+ theme_void()+
 draw_plot(plota_LSF,x = 0, y = 0.25, width = 0.878, height = 0.75)+
 draw_plot(season,x = wx1, y= 0.2, width = wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(lake,x = wx1, y=0.15, width = wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(depth,x = wx1, y = 0.1, width =wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(YEAR,x = wx1, y = 0.05, width =wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot(TEMP,x = wx1, y = 0, width =wx2, height = 0.05)+
  draw_plot_label(x = c(0.01,0.01,0.01,0.01,0.01), y = c(0.25,0.2,0.15,0.1,0.05), 
                     label =  c("Season","Lake","Depth","Year","Temp"),
                     hjust = 0, size = 7)+
  draw_plot_label(x=c(season_locx,lake_locx, strata_locx, year_locx, temp_locx), #temp
                  y=c(rep(0.25,2), #season x 2
                      rep(0.2,5), #lake x5
                      rep(0.15,10), #depth x 10
                      rep(0.1,9), #year x 9
                      rep(0.05,3)), #temp x3
                  label=c('Spring','Summer',
                          "SU","MI","HU","ER","ON",
                          names_depth,
                          "201","2","3","4","5","6","7","8","9",
                          'Low','High', 'NA'), 
                  color=c(season_colors["Spring"], season_colors["Summer"],
                          "#92809E", "#8cabea", "#64baaa","#FFC86A","#ee7061",
                          colors_depth,
                          "gray",'red','#A6CEE3','#B2DF8A',"brown","violet","purple","blue","black",
                          "blue","red","gray"),
                  hjust = 0, size = 6 )


  
  
ggsave("output/plots/LSF/LSF_sample_membership.pdf", LSF_structure, width = 7, height = 3, units = "in",dpi=600)
  
LSF_structure
```




### topic location
```{r}
#meta long
LSF_meta_long<- melt.data.frame(subset(LSF_meta,strat_status != "missing_data"), id.vars="Sample_num", variable_name="variable")
W_long_meta_LSF<-full_join(LSF_meta_long, W_long_LSF, by = "Sample_num")

p_season<-subset(W_long_meta_LSF,(variable == "SEASON") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(season_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=season_colors)+
      theme_bw()+
      labs(fill="Season",title="",y="Relative contribution",x= "Subcommunity")

p_season

p_lake<-subset(W_long_meta_LSF,(variable == "lake") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(lake_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=lake_colors)+
      theme_bw()+
      labs(fill="Lake",title="",y="",x= "")
p_lake

p_layer<-subset(W_long_meta_LSF,(variable == "new_depths_mdh2") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(depth2_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=depth2_colors)+
      theme_classic()+
      labs(fill="Layer",title="",y="",x= "")+
      theme(
        legend.position="right",  # Adjust if needed
        legend.key.size=unit(0.5, "cm"),  # Reduce legend key size
        legend.text=element_text(size=8),  # Smaller legend text
        legend.spacing.x=unit(0.2, "cm")  # Reduce spacing between legend items
      )



p_layer

p_year<-subset(W_long_meta_LSF,(variable == "YEAR") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(year_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=year_colors)+
      theme_bw()+
      labs(fill="Year",title="",y="",x= "")

p_year

p_status<-subset(W_long_meta_LSF,(variable == "strat_status2") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill= value.x))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=strat_colors)+
      labs(x="",y="", fill="Strat. Type")+
      theme_bw()

p_status

#sample prop - how much does this sc represent overall 
hold<-subset(W_long_meta_LSF, variable == "YEAR")  #can be any variable 
prop<-hold %>% group_by(topic) %>% dplyr::summarise(prop=round((sum(value.y)/sum(hold$value.y))*100))

p_dist<-ggplot(prop, aes(x=topic, y=prop, fill=topic)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label=paste0(round(prop, 1), "%")), 
            vjust=-0.5, 
            size=3, 
            fontface="bold") +  
  scale_fill_brewer(palette="Paired") +
  labs(x="", y="% of all sample distribution") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)))+
  theme(legend.position="none", 
        axis.text.y=element_blank(), 
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
        theme(axis.title=element_text(size=8, face="bold")))


figure = ggpubr::ggarrange(p_season, p_status, p_layer, p_lake, p_year,p_dist, ncol = 3, nrow = 2)

figure<-ggpubr::annotate_figure(figure, 
                        top = ggpubr::text_grob("LSF EUK: Topic Distribution across samples", color = "black", 
                                        face = "bold", size = 12))

ggsave("output/plots/LSF/LSF_membership.pdf", figure, width = 9, height = 5, units = "in",dpi=600)

figure

```


```{r}

p_lake<-subset(W_long_meta_LSF,(variable == "lake") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(lake_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=lake_colors)+
      scale_alpha_manual(values = c("yes" = 1, "no" = 0.3), guide = "none") +
      theme_classic()+
      labs(fill="Lake",title=expression(bold("B") * " Meta Overview"), 
           y="Relative contribution",x= "Subcommunity")
p_lake

p_layer<-subset(W_long_meta_LSF,(variable == "new_depths_mdh2") & (value.y>0)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(topic, value.y, fill=factor(value.x,levels=names(depth2_colors))))+
      geom_col(position="fill")+
      #geom_col()+
      scale_fill_manual(values=depth2_colors)+
      scale_alpha_manual(values = c("yes" = 1, "no" = 0.3), guide = "none") +
      theme_classic()+
      labs(fill="Layer",title="",y="Relative contribution",x= "Subcommunity")+
      theme(
        legend.position="right",  # Adjust if needed
        legend.key.size=unit(0.5, "cm"),  # Reduce legend key size
        legend.text=element_text(size=8),  # Smaller legend text
        legend.spacing.x=unit(0.2, "cm")  # Reduce spacing between legend items
      )

LSF_meta_plt<- ggpubr::ggarrange(p_lake, p_layer, ncol = 2, nrow = 1)
LSF_meta_plt
```



##prep meta
```{r}
prep_LSF   <- prep_topic_meta(W_LSF, LSF_meta)
```
## Limma
```{r}
limma_res_LSF_clr <- run_topic_clr_limma(prep_LSF$W_clean, prep_LSF$meta_clean)
sig_limma_clr_LSF  <- limma_res_LSF_clr$res_df  %>% filter(adj_p_value_by_term < 0.05)
sig_limma_clr_LSF 
```

```{r}
LSF_limma_crl <- plot_limma(
  sig_limma_clr_LSF ,
  topic_order = colnames(prep_LSF$W_clean),
  title       = expression(bold("C") * " Limma") ,
  meta_names  = meta_names,
  param_order = param_order
)

LSF_limma_crl
```


## RM + SHAP
```{r}
results_clr_LSF <- run_rf_oob_clr(prep_LSF$W_clean, prep_LSF$meta_clean)
results_clr_LSF
```
```{r}
# shap_dir_LSF <- run_shap_direction(
#   rf_models  = results_clr_LSF$rf_models,
#   meta_clean = prep_LSF$meta_clean,
#   nsim       = 500
# )

#save so we don't need to re-run everytime
saveRDS(shap_dir_LSF, file = "output/objects/LSF/shap_dir_LSF.rds")
shap_dir_LSF <- readRDS("output/objects/LSF/shap_dir_LSF.rds")

shap_dir_LSF
```

```{r}
# get the new names in the same order as param_order
new_order <- meta_names$new[match(param_order, meta_names$original)]

shap_dir_LSF2<- shap_dir_LSF %>%
  left_join(meta_names, by = c("Env" = "original")) %>%
  mutate(Env= coalesce(new, Env)) %>% 
  mutate(Env = factor(Env, levels = new_order))

LSF_rfshap<-plot_importance_direction_dot(
  shap_dir    = shap_dir_LSF2,
  rf_perf     = results_clr_LSF$rf_perf,
  top_n       = 15,
  param_order = new_order,
  title       = expression(bold("D") * " RM + SHAP")
)

#ggsave("../output/plots/FL/FL_RFshap.png", p, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in",dpi=600)

LSF_rfshap

```
#Summary Plot
```{r}
LSF_limma_crl_tight <- LSF_limma_crl + theme(plot.margin = margin(0, -2, 0, 0))
LSF_rfshap_tight    <- LSF_rfshap    + theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, -2))
bottom_right <- plot_grid(LSF_limma_crl_tight, LSF_rfshap_tight, ncol = 2,rel_widths = c(.8, 1.2))


right_col <- plot_grid(LSF_meta_plt, bottom_right, nrow = 2,rel_heights = c(.8, 1.3))

#grab ht_LSF as a grob if it's a ComplexHeatmap
ht_grob <- grid::grid.grabExpr(ComplexHeatmap::draw(ht_LSF))
LSF_summary <- plot_grid(ht_grob, right_col, 
                        ncol = 2,
                        align = "h",
                        axis = "tb",
                        rel_widths = c(1, 1.3))

ggsave("output/plots/LSF/LSF_summary.pdf", LSF_summary, width = 18, height = 8, units = "in",dpi=600)
LSF_summary
```


#____________
# All blocks
## Samples used
```{r}
library(ggtext)
```

```{r}
station_order=c("SU17M","SU08M","SU01M","SUFE","SU18","SU15","SU12","SU06",
                "HU54M","HU45M","HU15M","HUFE",
                "MI41M","MI27M","MI18M","MI40","MI50B","MIFO",
                "ER91M","ER78M","ER15M",
                "ON33M","ON41","ON55M","ON68B","ON64B")

plot_sample_heatmap <- function(X_meta, letter, name) {
  
  summary <- X_meta %>% 
    group_by(STATION_ID, SEASON, YEAR) %>% 
    dplyr::summarise(total_samples = n_distinct(Sample_num), .groups = "drop")
  
  summary$season_year <- paste(summary$SEASON, summary$YEAR, sep = "_")
  summary <- summary[, c("STATION_ID", "season_year", "total_samples")]
  
  station_order <- sort(unique(summary$STATION_ID))
  
  p <- ggplot(summary, 
              aes(x = season_year, y = factor(STATION_ID, levels = rev(station_order)), fill = total_samples)) +
    geom_tile() +
    coord_fixed() +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_markdown(angle = 90)) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightgreen", high = "darkgreen") +
    geom_text(aes(label = total_samples), color = "white", size = 3) +
    labs(fill = "# Samples", y = "Station ID", x = "Season",
         title = bquote(bold(.(letter)) ~ .(paste0(name, " n=", sum(summary$total_samples)))))
  
  return(p)
}

p_fl<- plot_sample_heatmap(FL_meta, "A", "Free Living Prokaryotes")
p_pa<- plot_sample_heatmap(PA_meta, "B", "Particle Associated Prokaryotes")
p_ssf<- plot_sample_heatmap(SSF_meta, "C", "Small Chloroplast")
p_lsf<- plot_sample_heatmap(LSF_meta, "D", "Large Chloroplast")


all_samples<-ggpubr::ggarrange(p_fl, p_pa, p_ssf, p_lsf, 
                                 ncol = 2, nrow = 2)

ggsave("output/plots/ALL/ALL_samples_used.pdf", all_samples, width = 12, height = 12, units = "in",dpi=600)
all_samples

```


## Structure summary 
```{r}
all_strcuture<-ggpubr::ggarrange(FL_structure,PA_structure,
                                 SSF_structure,LSF_structure,
                                 ncol = 2, nrow = 2)

ggsave("output/plots/ALL/ALL_structure.pdf", all_strcuture, width = 14, height = 6, units = "in",dpi=600)
all_strcuture
```

## GoMDE
```{r}
# Grab FL GoM-DE heatmap as grob
ht_grob_FL <- grid::grid.grabExpr({
  ComplexHeatmap::draw(ht_FL2)
  
  grid.text("Phylum", 
            x = unit(0.05, "npc"),  # tweak x
            y = unit(0.03, "npc"),  # tweak y — top
            gp = gpar(fontsize = 9, fontface = "bold"),
            just = c("left", "bottom"))
  
  grid.text("Order", 
            x = unit(0.75, "npc"),  # tweak x — right side
            y = unit(0.03, "npc"),   # vertical center
            gp = gpar(fontsize = 9, fontface = "bold"),
            just = c("right", "bottom"))
})


pheat2<-pheat+labs(title=expression(bold("B")*""))
p_swarm_pie2<-p_swarm_pie+labs(title=expression(bold("C")*""))

# Stack B and C on the right
right_col <- plot_grid(
  pheat2,  # panel B
  p_swarm_pie2,        # panel C
  nrow = 2,
  rel_heights = c(1, 1)  # B is shorter, C taller
)

# Combine A (left) and B/C stack (right)
gomde_fl <- plot_grid(
  ht_grob_FL, right_col,
  ncol = 2,
  align = "h",
  axis = "tb",
  rel_widths = c(1, 1)  # GoM-DE wider, adjust to taste
)

ggsave("output/plots/FL/GomDE_summary.pdf", gomde_fl, width = 14, height = 12, units = "in",dpi=600)

gomde_fl
```



## Env associations
```{r}
# Combine all blocks
limma_all <- bind_rows(
  sig_limma_clr_FL  %>% mutate(Block = "FL"),
  sig_limma_clr_PA  %>% mutate(Block = "PA"),
  sig_limma_clr_SSF %>% mutate(Block = "SSF"),
  sig_limma_clr_LSF %>% mutate(Block = "LSF")
) %>%
  mutate(Block = factor(Block, levels = c("FL", "PA", "SSF", "LSF")))

# Summarise per block × env variable
limma_summary <- limma_all %>%
  group_by(Block, term) %>%
  summarise(
    n_sig = n(),  # number of SCs with significant association
    mean_t = mean(t, na.rm = TRUE),  # dominant direction
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    direction_label = ifelse(mean_t > 0, "Positive", "Negative")
  ) %>%
  left_join(meta_names, by = c("term" = "original")) %>%
  mutate(term = coalesce(new, term))

# Plot
p_limma_summary <- ggplot(limma_summary,
                          aes(x = Block, y = term)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n_sig, color = direction_label), alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Positive" = "#E65100",
                                 "Negative" = "#1565C0"),
                     name = "Dominant\ndirection") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 10),
                        name = "Number of \n sig. SCs") +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("FL", "PA", "SSF", "LSF")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
       title = expression(bold("A") * " Limma Observed Associations")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey92"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

p_limma_summary 
```



```{r}
# Combine all blocks
rf_perf_all <- bind_rows(
  results_clr_FL$rf_perf  %>% mutate(Block = "FL"),
  results_clr_PA$rf_perf  %>% mutate(Block = "PA"),
  results_clr_SSF$rf_perf %>% mutate(Block = "SSF"),
  results_clr_LSF$rf_perf %>% mutate(Block = "LSF")
) %>%
  mutate(Block = factor(Block, levels = c("FL", "PA", "SSF", "LSF")))

# Block means
rf_means <- rf_perf_all %>%
  group_by(Block) %>%
  summarise(mean_R2 = mean(R2))

rf_means 

# Plot
p_q2 <- ggplot(rf_perf_all, aes(y = R2, x = Block, color = Block)) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0.15, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_crossbar(data = rf_means,
                aes(y = mean_R2, ymin = mean_R2, ymax = mean_R2),
                width = 0.5, linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("FL", "PA", "SSF", "LSF")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = sf_colors2) +
  labs(x = NULL, title = expression(bold("B") * " RF Prediction Power"),
       y = expression(Q^2 ~ "(OOB " * R^2 * ")")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())

p_q2

```
```{r}
# Combine all blocks
shap_all <- bind_rows(
  shap_dir_FL  %>% mutate(Block = "FL"),
  shap_dir_PA  %>% mutate(Block = "PA"),
  shap_dir_SSF %>% mutate(Block = "SSF"),
  shap_dir_LSF %>% mutate(Block = "LSF")
) %>%
  mutate(Block = factor(Block, levels = c("FL", "PA", "SSF", "LSF")))

shap_summary <- shap_all %>%
  mutate(direction_num = case_when(
    direction == "+" ~ 1,
    direction == "-" ~ -1,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>%
  group_by(Block, Env) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_imp  = mean(imp_scaled, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_pos  = mean(direction_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_topics  = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    dom_dir = case_when(
      prop_pos >= 0.70 ~ "+",
      prop_pos <= 0.30 ~ "−",
      TRUE ~ ""  # no dominant direction, leave blank
    )
  )

shap_plot <- shap_summary %>% 
  filter(show) %>%
  mutate(direction_label = ifelse(dir_color == "#E65100", 
                                   "Positive", "Negative")) %>%
  left_join(meta_names, by = c("Env" = "original")) %>%
  mutate(Env = coalesce(new, Env)) %>%
  mutate(Env = droplevels(factor(Env, levels = new_order)))

env_order <- shap_plot %>%
  group_by(Env) %>%
  summarise(total_imp = sum(mean_imp)) %>%
  arrange(total_imp) %>%
  pull(Env)

p_drivers <- ggplot(shap_plot, 
                    aes(x = Block, y = Env)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = direction_label, size = mean_imp), alpha = 0.9) +
  #geom_text(aes(label = dom_dir), 
  #          color = "white", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Positive" = "#E65100", 
                                 "Negative" = "#1565C0"),
                     name = "Dominant\ndirection") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(5, 14), 
                        name = "Mean importance\n(scaled)") +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("FL", "PA", "SSF", "LSF")) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = env_order) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = expression(bold("C") * " Predictive Importance")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey92"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5))

p_drivers 
```


```{r}
p<-ggpubr::ggarrange(p_limma_summary,p_q2, p_drivers,
                                 ncol = 3, nrow = 1, widths = c(1,.75,1))

ggsave("output/plots/ALL/meta_stats.pdf", p, width = 15, height = 6, units = "in",dpi=600)
p
```


## Distribution and entropy
### ASV
```{r}
ASV_distribution<- function(name, df, cutoff){
  #filter
  df_clean<- df %>% 
    filter (value > cutoff) %>% 
    group_by(ASV) %>% 
    dplyr::summarise(n_topics=n_distinct(topic)) %>% 
    ungroup() %>% 
    arrange(n_topics) %>% 
    mutate(sf=name)
  
  p<-ggplot(df_clean, aes(x=n_topics)) + 
    geom_bar()+
    theme_bw()+
    labs(x="found in this # of SCs", y="ASVs", title=paste(name, cutoff))+
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(df_clean$n_topics), max(df_clean$n_topics), by = 1))
  
  print(p)
  
   return(df_clean)
}

#ASV_distribution("PA",A_long_PA, 0.0001)
ASV_distribution("SSF_Chloro",A_long_SSF, 0.0001)
```

```{r}
fl_asvs_dist<-ASV_distribution("FL_Prok",A_long_FL, 0.0001)
pa_asvs_dist<-ASV_distribution("PA_Prok",A_long_PA, 0.0001)

ssf_asvs_dist<-ASV_distribution("SSF_Chloro",A_long_SSF, 0.0001)
lsf_asvs_dist<-ASV_distribution("LSF_Chloro",A_long_LSF, 0.0001)

join<-rbind(fl_asvs_dist,pa_asvs_dist,ssf_asvs_dist,lsf_asvs_dist)

#bacuse not all are observed
max_values <- c(FL_Prok = 10, PA_Prok = 10, SSF_Chloro = 9, LSF_Chloro = 11)

ecdf_data <- join %>%
  group_by(sf, n_topics) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_split(sf) %>%
  map_dfr(function(df_sf) {
    sf_name <- unique(df_sf$sf)
    df_sf %>%
      complete(n_topics = 1:max_values[[sf_name]], fill = list(n = 0)) %>%
      arrange(n_topics) %>%
      mutate(
        sf = sf_name,
        cdf = cumsum(n) / sum(n)
      )}) %>% 
  mutate(sf=factor(sf,levels=names(max_values)))

points <- ecdf_data %>%
  filter(n_topics %in% c(1,9))
points_adjusted <- points %>%
  filter(n_topics %in% c(9)) %>%
  mutate(cdf_adj = cdf + c(-.02,-.04,-.06,-.08)[as.numeric(factor(sf))])

p_asvs<- ggplot(ecdf_data, aes(x = n_topics, y = cdf, color = sf)) +
  geom_line(size = .5) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha=.7) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "# of SCs containing each ASV",
    y = "Cumulative Fraction of ASVs",
    color = "Dataset\n(p>0.0001)",
    title = expression(bold("B") ~ "")) + #ASV Distribution Across SCs
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = c(0.8, 0.3))+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(join$n_topics), 13, by = 1))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=sf_colors)+
  geom_text(
    data = subset(points,n_topics ==1),
    aes(label = n),
    hjust = 1.2, vjust = 0.5,
    size = 3,
    show.legend = FALSE)+
  geom_text(
      data = points_adjusted,
      aes(x = n_topics, y = cdf_adj, label = n, color = sf),
      hjust = -0.1,
      size = 3,
      show.legend = FALSE)+
  annotate("text", 
         x = .7, y = 0.6, 
         label = "n=", 
         size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = "black", hjust = 0)+
  guides(color="none")

p_asvs
```

### Samples
```{r}
sample_distribution<- function(name, df, cutoff){
  
  # reshape
  df$sample <- rownames(df) 
  
  df_long <- df %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = where(is.numeric),    
      names_to = "topic",
      values_to = "value"
    )
  
  # Reorder columns
  df_long <- df_long %>% select(sample, topic, value)
  
  # filter
  df_clean <- df_long %>% 
    filter(value > cutoff) %>% 
    group_by(sample) %>% 
    dplyr::summarise(n_topics = n_distinct(topic)) %>% 
    ungroup() %>% 
    arrange(n_topics) %>% 
    mutate(sf = name)
  
  p <- ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = n_topics)) + 
    geom_bar() +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(x = "found in this # of SCs", y = "samples", title = paste(name, cutoff)) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(df_clean$n_topics), max(df_clean$n_topics), by = 1))
  
  print(p)
  
  return(df_clean)
  
}

sample_distribution("FL",W_FL, 0.01)
#sample_distribution("LSF",W_LSF, 0.01)
```

```{r}
fl_sample_dist<-sample_distribution("FL_Prok",W_FL, 0.01)
pa_sample_dist<-sample_distribution("PA_Prok",W_PA, 0.01)

ssf_sample_dist<-sample_distribution("SSF_Chloro",W_SSF, 0.01)
lsf_sample_dist<-sample_distribution("LSF_Chloro",W_LSF, 0.01)

join<-rbind(fl_sample_dist,pa_sample_dist,ssf_sample_dist,lsf_sample_dist)

#bacuse not all are observed
max_values <- c(FL_Prok = 10, PA_Prok = 10, SSF_Chloro = 9, LSF_Chloro = 11)

ecdf_data <- join %>%
  group_by(sf, n_topics) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_split(sf) %>%
  map_dfr(function(df_sf) {
    sf_name <- unique(df_sf$sf)
    df_sf %>%
      complete(n_topics = 1:max_values[[sf_name]], fill = list(n = 0)) %>%
      arrange(n_topics) %>%
      mutate(
        sf = sf_name,
        cdf = cumsum(n) / sum(n)
      )}) %>% 
  mutate(sf=factor(sf,levels=names(max_values)))

p_samples<- ggplot(ecdf_data, aes(x = n_topics, y = cdf, color = sf)) +
  geom_line(size = .5) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha=.7) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "# of SCs observed in each sample",
    y = "Cumulative Fraction of Samples",
    color = "Dataset\n", #(p>0.01)
   title = expression(bold("C") ~ " ")) + #SC distribution across samples
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = c(0.8, 0.3))+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(join$n_topics), 13, by = 1))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=sf_colors)


p_samples
```


### Entropy
Higher entropy in a region (FL or PA) → May indicate an environment with greater microbial interaction, more functional redundancy, or dynamic shifts in community structure.

Lower entropy → Suggests strong environmental filtering, where certain conditions select for specific microbial groups, leading to more stable and distinct sub-communities.

```{r}
library(entropy)  # Install with install.packages("entropy") if needed
```

```{r}
# Function to compute Shannon entropy for each row (document)
compute_entropy <- function(topic_matrix) {
  apply(topic_matrix, 1, function(probabilities) {
    probabilities <- probabilities[probabilities > 0]  # Remove zero probabilities (to avoid log(0))
    -sum(probabilities * log(probabilities, base = 2))  # Shannon entropy formula
  })
}

# Compute entropy for each dataset
FL_entropy <- compute_entropy(W_FL)
PA_entropy <- compute_entropy(W_PA)
SSF_entropy <- compute_entropy(W_SSF)
LSF_entropy <- compute_entropy(W_LSF)

# Create a combined data frame for plotting
entropy_df <- data.frame(
  Entropy = c(FL_entropy, PA_entropy, SSF_entropy, LSF_entropy),
  Dataset = rep(c("FL_Prok", "PA_Prok", "SSF_Chloro", "LSF_Chloro"), 
                c(length(FL_entropy), length(PA_entropy), length(SSF_entropy), length(LSF_entropy)))
)

entropy_df$Dataset<-factor(entropy_df$Dataset, levels=c("FL_Prok", "PA_Prok", "SSF_Chloro", "LSF_Chloro"))

# Compute summary statistics (mean and standard deviation)
summary_df <- entropy_df %>%
  group_by(Dataset) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    MeanEntropy = mean(Entropy),
    SdEntropy = sd(Entropy)
  )

# Convert summary stats to text labels
summary_df <- summary_df %>%
  mutate(Label = paste0("Mean: ", round(MeanEntropy, 2), "\nSD: ", round(SdEntropy, 2)))


# Plot using ggplot with facet_wrap() for subplots
p_ent<-ggplot(entropy_df, aes(x=Entropy, fill=Dataset, color=Dataset)) +
  geom_density(alpha=0.6) +
  labs(x="Shannon Entropy Across Dataset", y="Density",
       title = expression(bold("D") ~ "")) + #Entropy of Sample Subcommunity Composition
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = c(0.9, 0.7),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=sf_colors) + 
  scale_color_manual(values=sf_colors) +
  #facet_wrap(~ Dataset)+
  guides(color="none", fill="none")+
  geom_text(data=subset(summary_df,Dataset == "FL_Prok"), 
            aes(x=2.1, y=.8, label=Label), color="#1f78b4", size=4, inherit.aes=FALSE)+
  geom_text(data=subset(summary_df,Dataset == "PA_Prok"), 
            aes(x=1, y=.75, label=Label), color="#b15928", size=4, inherit.aes=FALSE)+
  geom_text(data=subset(summary_df,Dataset == "SSF_Chloro"), 
            aes(x=0.2, y=.65, label=Label), color="#33a02c", size=4, inherit.aes=FALSE)+
  geom_text(data=subset(summary_df,Dataset == "LSF_Chloro"), 
            aes(x=2.6, y=.3, label=Label), color="#00441b", size=4, inherit.aes=FALSE)
p_ent
```
